qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: OAuth signup avatar_url SSRF via tampered omniauth registration completion form
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is an SSRF in the OAuth registration avatar import path (`POST /omniauth_registrations` -> `CreateOmniauthRegistration#attach_avatar` -> `URI.parse(...).open`) driven by attacker-controlled `user[avatar_url]`, while the advisory describes a stored XSS in the user name field that executes when another user visits a comment page.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding in this session identifies the advisory's stored XSS root cause in the user name field on comment-page rendering. The only accepted finding targets a separate OAuth avatar-fetch SSRF sink.
evidence: The advisory summary and details describe a stored XSS/code-execution issue in the user name field that impacts users who passively visit a comment page.

The accepted session finding is `oauth_avatar_ssrf`, which traces attacker control through `user[avatar_url]` in the omniauth registration completion flow and into `CreateOmniauthRegistration#attach_avatar`.
The finding's affected entrypoints are `POST /users/auth/:provider/callback` and `POST /omniauth_registrations`, not a comment-rendering path or a user-name output sink.
The vulnerability classes and sinks differ: stored XSS in rendered user names versus SSRF via server-side `open-uri` fetch of an avatar URL.
